steps.title: Find the cause of inefficiency
steps.content: The quotation explains that barter requires two people whose desires line up: each person must have what the other wants. That mutual fit is why barter takes so much time and becomes inefficient.

steps.title: Avoid shifted summaries
steps.content: B focuses on how barter can become monetary exchange. C and D describe barter or inefficiency without paraphrasing the quoted cause. The choice that restates the need for a precise alignment of desires directly explains the inefficiency. The answer is A.
